# Source

Vendored Codex `apply-patch` source and the MSP bridge crate belong here.

The source snapshot must include enough of the Codex Rust workspace to build the
patch engine without changing behavior. The Codex crate currently declares these
runtime dependencies:

- `codex-exec-server`
- `codex-utils-absolute-path`
- `similar`
- `thiserror`
- `tokio`
- `tree-sitter`
- `tree-sitter-bash`

Layout:

- `codex-rs/`: synchronized Codex Rust source snapshot, generated by
  `../Scripts/sync-codex-source.sh`.
- `codex-apply-patch-runtime/`: package wrapper whose library path points to
  `codex-rs/apply-patch/src/lib.rs`, preserving Codex parser/application
  source while allowing SDK-specific packaging dependencies.
- `codex-exec-server-compat/`: embedded packaging shim that exposes only the
  file-system trait/types used by Codex `apply-patch` without linking the full
  host exec server or Codex protocol stack.
- `msp-codex-apply-patch-bridge/`: MSP-owned Rust bridge crate. It depends on
  the Codex runtime wrapper through path dependencies and calls
  `codex_apply_patch::apply_patch` for behavior.
- `CODEX_SOURCE_PROVENANCE.txt`: generated source revision and scoped file
  hash evidence for the apply_patch runtime/proof surface.

Do not paste selected Rust functions into Swift or rewrite the parser. Updates
must be source-based and traceable back to the Codex files listed in the parent
README.
